description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: island state in the Caribbean Sea
description_wiki: Jamaica is an island country in the Caribbean, covering 10,990 square kilometres (4,240 sq mi).

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: largest city of the state of Louisiana, United States
description_wiki: New Orleans (commonly known as NOLA or  The Big Easy, among other nicknames) is a consolidated city-parish located along the Mississippi River in the U.S. state of Louisiana.

description: largest Christian Church, made up of 24 autonomous Churches in communion with the Pope, part of the Latin Church aka Latin Christianity aka western Christianity
group of fraternal organizations
description_wiki: Freemasonry (sometimes spelled Free-Masonry) consists of fraternal groups that trace their origins to medieval guilds of stonemasons.

description: county seat of Crawford County, Wisconsin, United States
description_wiki: Prairie du Chien ( PRAIR-ee doo SHEEN) is a city in Crawford County, Wisconsin, United States, and its county seat.

description: frontier conflicts between American, Canadian and European settlers and Indigenous peoples of the Americas
description_wiki: The American Indian Wars, also known as the American Frontier Wars, and the Indian Wars, were initially fought by European colonial empires, the United States, and briefly the Confederate States of America and Republic of Texas against various American Indian tribes in North America.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Michigan (  MISH-ig-ən) is a peninsular state in the Great Lakes region of the Upper Midwestern United States.

description: village in La Crosse County, Wisconsin
description_wiki: West Salem is a village in La Crosse County, Wisconsin, United States, along the La Crosse River.

description: city in and county seat of La Crosse County, Wisconsin, United States
description_wiki: La Crosse (  lə-KROSS) is a city in La Crosse County, Wisconsin, United States, and its county seat.

description: American author and humorist (1835–1910)
description_wiki: Samuel Langhorne Clemens (November 30, 1835 – April 21, 1910), known by the pen name Mark Twain, was an American writer, humorist, and essayist.

description: English writer and poet (1865–1936)
description_wiki: Joseph Rudyard Kipling ( RUD-yərd; 30 December 1865 – 18 January 1936) was an English journalist, novelist, poet and short-story writer.

description: German philosopher and theologian (1770–1831)
description_wiki: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel (27 August 1770 – 14 November 1831) was a German philosopher and a major figure in the tradition of German idealism.

description: American physical culturist and magazine publisher (1868–1955)
description_wiki: Bernarr Macfadden (born Bernard Adolphus McFadden, August 16, 1868 – October 12, 1955) was an American proponent of physical culture, a combination of bodybuilding with nutritional and health theories.

description: American magazine
description_wiki: Collier's was an American general-interest magazine founded in 1888 by Peter Fenelon Collier.

description: public research university in Madison, Wisconsin, USA
description_wiki: The University of Wisconsin–Madison (University of Wisconsin, Wisconsin, UW, UW–Madison, or simply Madison) is a public land-grant research university in Madison, Wisconsin, United States.

surface_forms: Louisiana State University at Baton Mug
aliases: Louisiana State University and Agricultural and Mechanical College
description: public university in Baton Rouge, Louisiana, USA
description_wiki: Louisiana State University and Agricultural and Mechanical College, commonly referred to as Louisiana State University (LSU), is an American public land-grant research university in Baton Rouge, Louisiana, United States.

description: public research university in Urbana and Champaign, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: The University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (UIUC, U of I, Illinois, or University of Illinois) is a public land-grant research university in the Champaign–Urbana metropolitan area, Illinois, United States.

aliases: 1st Baron Tennyson of Aldworth and Freshwater Alfred Tennyson
description: British Poet Laureate (1809–1892)
description_wiki: Alfred Tennyson, 1st Baron Tennyson  (; 6 August 1809 – 6 October 1892) was an English poet.

description_wiki: The Philippines, officially the Republic of the Philippines, is an archipelagic country in Southeast Asia.

description: largest island of the Philippines
description_wiki: Luzon ( loo-ZON, Tagalog: [luˈson]) is the largest and most populous island in the Philippines.

description: island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description_wiki: China, officially the People's Republic of China (PRC), is a country in East Asia.

description_wiki: Islam is an Abrahamic monotheistic religion based on the Quran and the teachings of Muhammad.
